_description: Error handling strategies for AI-Hydro tools and workflows

gauge_not_found.error: ValueError: Gauge ID not found or no data available
causes: Gauge exists but has no data for specified period
solutions: Verify gauge ID is 8-digit string with leading zeros
Check gauge on USGS website: https://waterdata.usgs.gov/nwis/
Adjust date range to match data availability
Retry after brief delay if API issue

causes: Invalid DEM data at gauge location
solutions: Check DEM coverage includes gauge location
Manually verify gauge location on map

data_extraction_error.error: ValueError: No valid data within watershed boundary
causes: Boundary doesn't overlap with data source
solutions: Check CRS compatibility between boundary and data
Inspect data coverage at gauge location

causes: Watershed too large for available RAM
solutions: Close unused variables to free memory

timeout_error.error: TimeoutError: Data download exceeded time limit

basic_try_except.description: Basic error handling with informative messages

retry_logic.description: Retry operations that may fail transiently

graceful_degradation.description: Continue with partial results if some operations fail

logging_for_debugging.example:
import logging
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
try:
    logger.info(f'Processing gauge {gauge_id}')
    result = tool_function(params)
    logger.info('Processing completed successfully')
except Exception as e:
    logger.error(f'Processing failed: {e}', exc_info=True)
    raise

data_inspection: Print intermediate results to verify correctness
Check data shapes and types: print(data.shape, data.dtype)
Plot data to visualize patterns and anomalies
step_by_step_execution: Break complex workflows into individual steps
Run each step independently before chaining
Use interactive Python session (IPython/Jupyter) for exploration
parameter_validation: Check for None or empty values
error_reproduction: Record exact error message and stack trace
Try with different input data to isolate cause

checkpoint_and_resume.description: Save progress to recover from failures
implementation: Save results after each major step
Check for existing results before reprocessing
Use unique filenames to avoid overwrites
Implement resume logic from last checkpoint

fallback_methods.description: Use alternative approaches when preferred method fails
implementation: Try coarser resolution if high-resolution fails
Use alternative data source if primary unavailable
Apply simpler method if complex one fails
Skip optional computations if errors occur

partial_results.description: Return what succeeded even if some parts failed
implementation: Mark failed attributes with None or NaN

input_validation: Check data types, ranges, and formats
Test with edge cases during development
defensive_programming: Set reasonable defaults for optional parameters
testing: Write unit tests for individual functions

required_information: Complete error message and stack trace
